title: support cross-module task invocation?
body:
Hi,

I am wondering how much work it would take to support invocation of tasks from outside of the module that defines them. Here is a minimal example:

```verilog
module counter;
  reg [31:0] cnt = 0;
  task increment; begin
    cnt = cnt + 1;
  end endtask
endmodule

module top(input wire clk);
  counter C();
  always @(posedge clk) begin
    C.increment(); // yosys: ERROR: Can't resolve task name `\C.increment'.
  end
endmodule
```

[Internets](https://stackoverflow.com/questions/37624934/how-to-call-tasks-from-a-separate-module-in-verilog) tell me that this is not supposed to work, but it does in `iverilog`, and I would like to use it in yosys for prototyping in the context of a larger system.

I would be be happy to try my hand at implementing this, but first I am reaching out to hear whether there are any gotchas that would make this much more work than replacing code like https://github.com/YosysHQ/yosys/blob/1706798f4e595266a8758ae6d0ff9d978299de10/frontends/ast/simplify.cc#L2231 with... something recursive.

[Here](https://gist.github.com/andres-erbsen/7ae70c455eca1b0da3ced0636e94f6a2) is a more complete example of the desired behavior.

Thanks,
Andres
